title: sambucus nigra f. pyramidalis
intro: Sambucus nigra f. pyramidalis, also known as black elderberry or elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral compounds, particularly in its berries, flowers, and leaves. The health benefits of Sambucus nigra f. pyramidalis include relief from cold and flu symptoms, reduction of inflammation and pain, and support for immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues and viral infections. Sambucus nigra f. pyramidalis is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and its berries are also used to make jams, jellies, and wines.
